mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive return frequency drops below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates robust consumer demand and construction activity, generating positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint signal produces negative returns in more than 55% of cases over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the index of industrial production reflects weakening manufacturing output and supply-side slack, leading to negative short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Signal fails to predict direction with accuracy greater than 52% in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained consumer price inflation above the central bank target erodes real corporate earnings and forces tighter financial conditions, producing negative equity returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 65% of threshold-crossing events.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls demonstrate expanding economic capacity and rising household income, supporting corporate revenue growth and positive market returns.
failure_condition: Rank correlation between payroll growth and forward returns falls below 0.05 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between slowing GDP growth and declining retail sales confirms weakening aggregate demand, triggering negative equity returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarterly growth falls below 1.5 percent
failure_condition: Combined signal yields positive returns in more than 58% of triggered observations.

mechanism_narrative: A reversal in industrial production from contraction to expansion signals inventory restocking and renewed factory activity, driving positive short-term market returns.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month change turns positive after 2 consecutive declines
failure_condition: Positive return frequency drops below 48% following the specified production reversal pattern.
